title: Candidate-conditioned pairwise multiclass scorer

queries: graph llm candidate conditioned scoring multiclass ranking pairwise interface autocomplete mrr
graph rag pairwise candidate interaction scorer structured classification interface

papers.title: Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey
papers.claim: Graph-enhanced generation quality depends on aligning retrieval, evidence organization, and downstream decision interfaces.
papers.relevance: Supports moving from a shallow label-view choice to a more explicit candidate-conditioned scoring interface.

papers.title: G-Retriever: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Graph Question Answering
papers.claim: Graph evidence must be consumed through an interface matched to the downstream decision problem.
papers.relevance: Supports introducing candidate-specific interactions between prompt evidence and candidate representations for autocomplete-style ranking.

papers.title: Can we Soft Prompt LLMs for Graph Learning Tasks?
papers.claim: Prompt-side graph/task interface design can dominate gains once encoder-side modifications saturate.
papers.relevance: Supports a stronger scoring-interface redesign after class-id unification and static dual-view fusion both remained globally insufficient.

evidence.why_now: EXP081 showed that class-id label-interface unification is directionally helpful but too weak, while EXP084 showed that static dual-view fusion still leaves item-incoterms in the same regressive band. EXP087 and EXP090 also reduced the value of packaging-nearby reruns. The next justified step is therefore a candidate-conditioned scorer that lets the prompt representation interact with each candidate more explicitly.

hypothesis: The current scorer family is still too static: it compares one prompt-conditioned sample representation against mostly static candidate embeddings. For autocomplete multiclass tasks, adding candidate-conditioned interaction terms between the prompt representation and each candidate view should preserve salt-side ranking discrimination better than static verbalizer selection or a single shared dual-view fusion gate.
notes: First implementation target: for autocomplete multiclass tasks only, compute candidate-specific interaction features from prompt x class-id view, prompt x raw-label view, and class-id/raw-label disagreement, then score each candidate with a lightweight pairwise scorer.
Do not mix new loss terms, sampling tweaks, or packaging changes into the first implementation.
Launched on 2026-05-07 through the local Stage 3 launcher.
First stable orchestrator status check shows all three windows up; user-churn and user-ltv completed DB load, while item-incoterms remained in early salt startup.
Completed on 2026-05-07 with global verdict: failed.

Outcome: user-ltv improved above the stored strict baseline and item-incoterms improved relative to recent candidate-aware runs, but user-churn regressed and salt still stayed below the strict MRR baseline.
